metadata.title: Roam control mapping — supports evidence for governance frameworks
metadata.remarks: This Control Mapping maps to / supports evidence for the listed controls. It does not certify compliance and is not a substitute for an authorising body's assessment. Roam emits evidence packets that customers present to their auditor; the auditor remains the authority.

maps.remarks: Roam's run ledger maps to EU AI Act Article 12 record-keeping by capturing timestamp, actor, and action for every recorded agent event.

props.name: claim
props.value: Each AI-agent action is recorded with timestamp and actor.

maps.remarks: Each PR bundle carries a run_id, agent identifier, and intent string, which supports evidence for traceability of AI-generated changes.

props.name: claim
props.value: Every AI-generated code change is traceable to a run_id and agent.

maps.remarks: `roam runs verify` produces an audit-ready record of HMAC-chain integrity, which maps to Article 12 expectations for tamper-evident activity logs.

props.name: claim
props.value: The audit-trail HMAC chain verifies with no gaps in event sequence.

maps.remarks: The PR bundle's `context_read` manifest, combined with a recorded preflight verdict on the changed subjects, supports evidence for the internal AI-change-policy expectation that the agent reviewed relevant context before editing.

props.name: claim
props.value: Agent read relevant context before edit.

maps.remarks: Where the bundle declared `tests_required` and recorded a non-empty `tests_run` with at least one passing entry, Roam supports evidence for the internal AI-change-policy test gate.

props.name: claim
props.value: Required tests were declared and at least one passed before merge.

maps.remarks: For changes whose affected_symbols include a blast_radius above the site's configured threshold, the bundle's `risks[]` rationale, combined with a named authorizer on the run, maps to the internal AI-change policy's high-blast review requirement.

props.name: claim
props.value: High-blast-radius changes have an additional approval signal.

maps.remarks: Roam's mode substrate maps to ISO/IEC 42001 expectations for operational boundaries by recording the active mode at every event and the explicit mode-switch events that change it.

props.name: claim
props.value: Agents operate within declared modes (read_only, safe_edit, migration, autonomous_pr).

maps.remarks: Where the PR bundle's `context_read.commands_run` includes a preflight or impact invocation, Roam supports evidence for ISO/IEC 42001 AI-risk assessment requirements.

props.name: claim
props.value: A pre-edit blast-radius analysis exists for changes the agent later applied.

maps.remarks: For runs that invoked `migration` or `autonomous_pr`, the ledger's mode-switch event records a named authorizer and rationale; this maps to ISO/IEC 42001 governance-review requirements.

props.name: claim
props.value: Sensitive edits above safe_edit are linked to a named human authorizer.

maps.remarks: Roam's pr-bundle and run ledger maps to NIST AI 600-1 GV-1.2-001 value-chain transparency by recording actor, run, and content-hash provenance for every AI-assisted code change. Scope note: GV-1.2-001 targets model version and training-data provenance; roam covers code-change provenance (same structure, different subject).

props.name: claim
props.value: Every AI-agent code change carries actor, run, and content-hash provenance.

maps.remarks: Roam's constitution-gate evaluation and mode substrate (with read_only blocking the write surface) supports evidence for NIST AI 600-1 GV-1.3-006/007 stop-build authority. Scope note: gate enforcement is repo-local; no external deployment pipeline integration today.

props.name: claim
props.value: Constitution gates and mode substrate block writes on unacceptable risk.

maps.remarks: Roam's cumulative mode substrate (read_only / safe_edit / migration / autonomous_pr) supports evidence for NIST AI RMF GOVERN function requirements by making each agent's action surface explicit.

props.name: claim
props.value: Each AI agent has documented capability boundaries via declared mode.

maps.remarks: The PR bundle's `risks[]` array, together with per-symbol `blast_radius` values, maps to NIST AI RMF MEASURE function expectations for risk identification and quantification.

props.name: claim
props.value: Run-level risk verdicts and per-symbol risk entries are recorded.

maps.remarks: Roam records each policy-relevant event (mode switch, gate evaluation, lease claim) on the chained ledger; this maps to NIST AI RMF MANAGE function expectations for decision context.

props.name: claim
props.value: Policy decisions are recorded with timestamp and decision context.

maps.remarks: Roam's ChangeEvidence (actor_refs, run_ids, commit_sha, context_read) maps to the NIST SP 800-218A PW.3.2 provenance definition for AI-assisted code changes. Scope note: PW.3.2 targets model training-data provenance; roam covers code-change provenance (same structure, different subject).

props.name: claim
props.value: AI-assisted code changes carry the provenance fields defined in SP 800-218A PW.3.2.

maps.remarks: Where the PR bundle records a roam critique invocation before merge, Roam maps to NIST SP 800-218A PW.6 code-review requirements for AI-generated output. Scope note: critique applies uniformly today; no explicit "AI-generated" flag on reviewed hunks.

props.name: claim
props.value: Pre-merge critique gates AI-generated diff before merge.

maps.remarks: Roam's mode substrate (cumulative read_only / safe_edit / migration / autonomous_pr) and permit records supports evidence for NIST SP 800-218A PO.3.2 developer-authorization requirements. Scope note: authorization is repo-local; no org-level IdP/SSO integration today.

props.name: claim
props.value: Write-surface actions are gated by declared mode and named permit.

maps.remarks: Roam's mode substrate (cumulative read_only / safe_edit / migration / autonomous_pr) and constitution-gate evaluations, recorded on `ChangeEvidence.authority_refs[]`, maps to SLSA SRC-L2 authority requirements for protected-branch change controls. Scope note: authority enforcement is repo-local; SCM-level branch protections remain the SCM's responsibility.

props.name: claim
props.value: Protected-branch actions are gated by declared mode and recorded authority.

maps.remarks: Roam's HMAC-chained run ledger and pr-bundle (actor_refs, commit_sha, run_id, per-event timestamps) maps to SLSA SRC-L2 contemporaneous source-provenance requirements. Scope note: the ledger HMAC is locally tamper-evident; external transparency anchoring is the SRC-L3 lift covered by `SLSA_SRC_L3_VSA_PREDICATE` below.

props.name: claim
props.value: Each revision carries contemporaneous actor, timestamp, and revision-id provenance.

maps.remarks: Roam's `pr-bundle emit --slsa-l3 --sign --keyless` (and the parallel `cga emit --also-vsa` surface) wraps `ChangeEvidence` in an in-toto v1 Statement carrying `predicateType: https://slsa.dev/verification_summary/v1`, signed via Fulcio + Rekor. This supports evidence for SLSA SRC-L3 verification-summary attestation requirements. Scope note: roam emits the VSA evidence; external slsa-verifier consumers issue the level claim.

props.name: claim
props.value: A Verification Summary Attestation in SLSA v1 VSA shape is emitted with the change.

maps.remarks: Each emitted PR bundle ties its commit_sha to a run_id and named agent, which supports evidence for SOC 2 CC8.1 change-authorization records.

props.name: claim
props.value: Each commit links to a documented run_id and agent actor.

maps.remarks: Where the PR bundle records a `roam critique` or `roam impact` invocation before merge, Roam supports evidence for SOC 2 CC8.1 change-review requirements.

props.name: claim
props.value: Pre-merge review evidence exists in the PR bundle (critique + impact).

maps.remarks: The PR bundle composes commit_sha, run_id, context_read, and tests_run into an audit-ready record that maps to SOC 2 CC8.1 traceability requirements.

props.name: claim
props.value: A commit links to its run, the context the agent read, and the tests it ran.

resources.title: Roam ChangeEvidence
resources.description: Portable evidence packet emitted per code change. Source of evidence that maps to the listed governance controls.

resources.title: SLSA v1.2 Source Track Level 2 (history + provenance)
SLSA v1.2 Source Track Level 3 (continuous technical controls)
